do.e: I do my homework every night.

did.d: To take action or complete a task
did.e: The boy did his homework before playing outside.

day.e: I usually play outside on sunny days.

down.e: The puppy jumped down from the couch.

does.d: The way to say someone takes an action
does.e: The dog does tricks for treats.

during.d: Throughout the time of an event
during.e: I play outside during the day.

days.d: The times from morning to night
days.e: We have school for five days a week.

different.e: My brother and I have different favorite colors.

doing.e: I'm doing my chores around the house.

done.e: I'm done with my homework for the day.

de.e: This is de cat's favorite toy.

due.d: Something that is owed or expected

development.d: the process of growing and improving
development.e: The new playground is part of the community's development plan.

data.d: Information that is collected and used
data.e: The scientist gathered data to help understand the problem.

deal.e: We made a good deal at the store today.

department.d: A part of a larger organization that does a certain job
department.e: The science department at my school has a lab with cool experiments.

died.d: When someone or something stops living
died.e: The old dog died and we were very sad.

dr.d: A person who helps people when they are sick
dr.e: I went to see the doctor for a checkup.

date.d: A special day or time to meet someone
date.e: My parents are going on a date night tonight.

decision.d: A choice you make about what to do.
decision.e: The family had to make a decision about where to go on vacation.

design.d: The way something is planned and made
design.e: The designer made a cool design for my new shirt.

die.e: The old plant started to die without water.

david.d: A common name for a boy or man.
david.e: David played soccer with his friends after school.

difficult.d: Something that is hard to do or understand
difficult.e: Solving that math problem was really difficult for me.

director.d: The person in charge of a group or organization
director.e: The movie director told the actors where to stand.

drive.d: To control a car and make it move
drive.e: I like to drive my bicycle to the park.

decided.d: To make a choice or a judgment about something
decided.e: I decided to go to the park with my friends.

december.d: The last month of the year.
december.e: My birthday is in December, so I get presents!

door.d: The opening in a wall that you can go through to enter or leave a room.
door.e: I opened the door and walked into my house.

daily.e: I do my chores daily to keep my room clean.

dog.d: A furry animal that is a popular pet
dog.e: My dog loves to play fetch with me

dark.d: The opposite of light, when it's nighttime
dark.e: I can see the stars in the dark sky at night.

daughter.d: A female child of a parent
daughter.e: My daughter loves reading bedtime stories with me.

described.d: To explain what something is like
described.e: The teacher described the science project we need to do.

developed.d: to grow or improve over time
developed.e: The new park has developed into a nice place for families to play.

difference.d: The way two things are not the same
difference.e: There is a big difference between a cat and a dog.

double.d: When there are two of the same thing
double.e: I have double the number of pencils that you have.

deep.d: Going a long way down or back
deep.e: The ocean is very deep in some places.

despite.d: Even though something else is true
despite.e: Despite the rain, the kids still went outside to play.

direction.d: The way something is going or pointing
direction.e: The map showed the direction to the park.

designed.d: Made or planned for a specific purpose
designed.e: The new toy was designed to be fun and easy to use.

direct.d: Going straight to the point without going the long way
direct.e: I need to take the direct route to get to the park on time.

drop.e: I accidentally dropped my pencil on the floor.

disease.d: an illness that can make a person sick
disease.e: The doctor said the child had a serious disease.

degree.d: A level of education or amount of something
degree.e: My teacher has a college degree in science.

dad.e: I asked my dad to help me with my homework.

dance.d: to move your body to music in a rhythmic way
dance.e: The kids danced happily at the school party.

directly.d: straight, without stopping or going the long way
directly.e: I walked directly to the park after school.

drink.d: a liquid that you swallow, like water or juice
drink.e: I like to drink a glass of milk with my lunch.

doctor.d: a person who helps when you are sick or hurt
doctor.e: I went to the doctor because I had a sore throat.

doubt.d: a feeling of not being sure about something
doubt.e: I had doubts about trying the new food, but it was delicious.

dream.d: something you imagine or think about while sleeping
dream.e: I had a funny dream about flying on a magic carpet.

damage.e: The storm caused a lot of damage to the trees.

details.d: small facts or information about something
details.e: The book gave a lot of details about how the story happened.

district.d: A certain area or section of a town or city
district.e: I live in the downtown district of the city.

driving.d: Operating a vehicle, like a car or truck.
driving.e: My mom is driving us to the park.

defense.d: Actions to protect yourself or something else
defense.e: The goalie made a great defense to block the shot.

demand.d: To ask for something strongly or require it
demand.e: The teacher will demand that students study hard.

definitely.d: Something that is sure to happen
definitely.e: I will definitely finish my homework tonight.

division.d: The act of splitting something into smaller parts
division.e: The teacher showed us the division of the numbers on the board.

distance.d: The space between two places or things.
distance.e: The school is a long distance from my house.

digital.e: The class learned about digital art on the computers.

dude.d: A friendly way to address a boy or man
dude.e: Hey dude, want to play catch later?

draw.d: To make a picture using pencils or pens
draw.e: I like to draw pictures of my family.

dear.d: Someone or something that is very special or important to you
dear.e: My dear friend is the best person I know.

discussion.d: When people talk about something and share their thoughts and ideas
discussion.e: The class had a discussion about their favorite books.

decide.e: I need to decide what to eat for lunch.

duty.d: Something you have to do, like a job or task.
duty.e: It is my duty to clean my room every day.

dangerous.d: Something that could hurt or harm you
dangerous.e: The rocky path was dangerous for the hikers to walk on.

democratic.d: Relating to a government run by the people
democratic.e: Our country is a democratic nation with elected leaders.

determined.d: When someone really wants to do something and won't stop trying
determined.e: The athlete was determined to win the big race.

develop.d: To grow or change into something new and better
develop.e: Over time, the new business will develop and grow.

dinner.d: The main meal of the day, usually eaten in the evening.
dinner.e: We had a delicious dinner with our family.

drinking.d: The act of swallowing liquids like water or juice.
drinking.e: She was thirsty and started drinking her water.

discovered.d: To find or learn about something for the first time.
discovered.e: The explorer discovered a new species of animal in the forest.

domestic.d: Relating to the home or family.
domestic.e: Cooking and cleaning are domestic chores around the house.

description.d: Words that tell what something or someone is like.
description.e: The description of the lost puppy helped find it.

decisions.d: Choices you make about what to do
decisions.e: I had to make a decision about my homework.

dry.e: The towel is dry after I used it.

dogs.d: A common pet animal that is loyal and friendly
dogs.e: My dog loves to run and play in the backyard.

dont.d: A way to say you should not do something
dont.e: My mom says I shouldn't stay up too late, so I don't watch TV after bedtime.

dropped.d: To let something fall down or fall to the ground
dropped.e: I accidentally dropped my pencil and had to pick it up from the floor.

dress.d: A piece of clothing that covers the upper and lower body
dress.e: The girl wore a pretty blue dress to the party.

definition.d: An explanation of what a word or thing means.
definition.e: The dictionary gives the definition of each word.

device.d: A machine or tool that is used for a specific purpose.
device.e: I use my tablet device to read books and watch videos.

debt.d: Money you owe to someone else.
debt.e: The family has a lot of debt from their car loan.

defence.d: Protecting yourself or something from harm.
defence.e: The team played great defence to stop the other team.

determine.d: To decide or figure out what to do.
determine.e: The teacher helped determine the best way to solve the problem.

driver.d: A person who operates a vehicle, like a car or bus.
driver.e: The driver followed the directions carefully.

defined.d: to explain or describe what something is
defined.e: The teacher defined the new vocabulary word for the class.

developing.d: To start growing and changing into something new
developing.e: The baby is developing and getting bigger every day.

discuss.d: To talk about something with someone else
discuss.e: The teacher and students will discuss the story in class.

display.d: To show something so people can see it
display.e: The museum will display the new art exhibit this weekend.

draft.d: The first version of something before the final one
draft.e: I need to write a draft of my essay before I turn it in.

debate.d: A discussion where people share different opinions
debate.e: The class had a debate about the new school rules.

destroyed.d: To damage something so badly that it cannot be used anymore
destroyed.e: The storm destroyed the old barn.

drunk.e: The thirsty dog drank the water in his bowl.

download.d: to copy a file from the internet to your device
download.e: I downloaded a new game to play on my tablet.

drama.d: a story with exciting events and strong emotions
drama.e: My favorite TV show is a drama about a family.

dying.e: The flowers are dying because they need more water.

danger.d: Something that could cause harm or injury.
danger.e: The big waves at the beach were a danger to the swimmers.

declared.d: When you say something is true or official.
declared.e: The teacher declared the test scores would be given out tomorrow.

documents.e: The family kept all their important documents in a safe place.

daniel.d: A boy's name that means 'God is my judge'
daniel.e: Daniel is a kind boy who helps others.

des.d: A shortened name for the city of Des Moines in the state of Iowa
des.e: Des is the capital city of Iowa.

desire.e: I desire to win the spelling bee.

directed.d: To tell someone what to do
directed.e: The teacher directed the students to sit down.

dealing.d: the act of taking care of or handling something
dealing.e: I am dealing with a lot of homework this week.

describe.d: to say what something is like
describe.e: Can you describe the color of your new shirt?

delivered.d: When something is brought to a person or place
delivered.e: The package was delivered to my front door.

democracy.d: A type of government where the people choose their leaders
democracy.e: In a democracy, the people vote to elect their leaders.

depending.d: Based on or influenced by something else
depending.e: I will do my chores depending on how much time I have.

deputy.d: A person who helps the leader or boss of a group
deputy.e: The deputy principal helps the principal run the school.

devices.d: Tools or machines that are used for a specific purpose
devices.e: I use many different devices to do my homework.

diet.d: The types of food a person regularly eats
diet.e: Eating lots of fruits and vegetables is part of a healthy diet.

differences.d: The ways two or more things are not the same
differences.e: There are many differences between cats and dogs.

don.d: A short way to say 'do not' or 'does not'
don.e: I don't want to eat broccoli for dinner.

dedicated.d: Dedicated means to be very committed and hardworking at something.
dedicated.e: The athlete was dedicated to training every day.

depends.d: Depends means that something is decided by or relying on other factors.
depends.e: Whether we can go to the park depends on the weather.

detail.d: A detail is a small, specific part or piece of information.
detail.e: The artist added lots of small details to the painting.

dreams.d: Dreams are thoughts or images in your mind when you are sleeping.
dreams.e: I had a fun dream about going to the zoo.

du.d: Du is a name that can be used for a boy or a girl.
du.e: My friend Du has a new bike.

drawn.d: Pulled or moved to a certain place
drawn.e: The puppy was drawn to the sound of food.

dating.d: When two people spend time together to get to know each other.
dating.e: My parents went on a date to the park.

di.d: A small word that means the same as 'the'.
di.e: Di cat likes to play with her toys.

dollar.d: The main type of money used in many countries.
dollar.e: I saved up my dollars to buy a new toy.

drawing.d: Making a picture by hand using a pencil or pen.
drawing.e: I like to draw pictures of my family.

depression.d: A serious mental health condition that makes people feel very sad.
depression.e: Some people struggle with depression, but there are ways to get help.

dirty.d: When something is not clean and has spots or stains.
dirty.e: The child's face was dirty after eating ice cream.

divided.d: When something is split into different parts.
divided.e: The sandwich was divided into two halves.

doors.d: The entrances to a room or building.
doors.e: The doors to the school were open.

degrees.d: A way to measure temperature or how much of something there is
degrees.e: The weather is 75 degrees outside today.

del.d: A short way to write the word 'delete'
del.e: I need to del that message I sent by mistake.

deliver.d: To take something to where it needs to go
deliver.e: The mail carrier will deliver the package to my house.

depth.d: How deep or far down something goes
depth.e: The water in the pool has a depth of 4 feet.

deserve.d: To earn or be entitled to something
deserve.e: I deserve a treat for cleaning my room.

discussed.d: To talk about something in detail
discussed.e: We discussed our plans for the weekend.

duke.d: A person with a high rank in a royal family
duke.e: The duke owns a big castle.

dan.e: Dan played video games with his friends.

deals.e: The store had great deals on toys.

defeat.d: To win against someone or something.
defeat.e: The team worked hard to defeat their opponents.

delivery.d: Bringing something to a person or place.
delivery.e: The delivery person brought the package to the house.

dates.d: the numbers that show the day, month, and year
dates.e: The calendar showed the dates for the whole month.

deaths.d: when someone or something stops living
deaths.e: The news reported the sad news of the deaths in the accident.

decades.e: The old house had stood for decades before it was torn down.

driven.d: determined to do something and working hard to make it happen
driven.e: The athlete was driven to win the big game.

drivers.d: people who operate vehicles like cars, trucks, or buses
drivers.e: The bus drivers took the children to school each day.

dancing.d: Moving your body to music in a fun way
dancing.e: The kids danced and sang along to the music at the party.

destroy.d: To completely break or ruin something
destroy.e: The tornado destroyed the old barn on the farm.

doctors.d: People who help make you feel better when you are sick or hurt
doctors.e: The doctor checked the boy's broken arm and said it was healing well.

document.d: A paper or file that has important information written on it
document.e: She had to sign the important document to get a new driver's license.

detailed.d: When something has a lot of small parts or information
detailed.e: The map had a very detailed picture of the city.

decade.e: The family has lived here for over a decade.

defend.d: to protect something from harm or attack
defend.e: The team had to defend their goal to win the game.

demands.d: to ask for something in a firm way
demands.e: The customer demanded a refund for the broken product.

directors.d: people who are in charge of a company or organization
directors.e: The directors of the company made the important decisions.

discovery.d: finding something new or learning about something for the first time
discovery.e: The discovery of a new planet was very exciting for the scientists.

dna.d: the material inside living things that contains information about how they grow and work
dna.e: Humans have DNA that makes us unique from other animals.

drew.d: to make a picture by using a pencil, pen, or other tool
drew.e: The artist drew a beautiful picture of a flower.

dumb.e: The dumb dog couldn't bark to let us know it was hungry.
